key_insight: PASS is a middleware that reshapes step-level process signals so GRPO can assign credit more cleanly, reducing contamination, granularity mismatch, and length-bias traps.
engineering_relevance: It offers a drop-in way to make process-supervised RL more stable and transferable across different signal sources, tasks, and group-standardization choices.
method: Advantage Fusion: standardize process, outcome, and format streams separately within each group.
Chunk-by-Value: form credit chunks from signal homogeneity and broadcast reward within each chunk.
Divide-Length: replace cumulative return with average value-density to avoid length inflation or premature truncation.
evidence_signal: The authors test PASS on mathematical reasoning with a learned PRM and on multi-hop QA with on-policy-distillation KL signals, and report consistent pass@1 gains over GRPO baselines across two domains and two standardization operators.
watch_item: Because PASS depends on how step-level signals are chunked and normalized, implementation quality and signal calibration may strongly affect the final credit assignment behavior.
paper_title: Process Advantage Signal Shaping: A Paradigm-Agnostic Middleware for Process-Supervised RL in LLM Reasoners
